refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional obligation: supporting teleoperation with a specified communication quality metric (packet loss limit). It does not combine multiple requirements or specify implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication quality monitoring and adaptive control mechanism to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service for teleoperation
Incorporate error detection and correction protocols to reduce effective packet loss
Use buffering and retransmission strategies to handle packet loss dynamically
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication quality monitoring and adaptive control mechanism to maintain packet loss within limits

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on monitoring and adaptive control, which logically isolates communication quality management from other system functions, supporting independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and stable enough for adaptive control to be effective
Risk that adaptive mechanisms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes that packet loss can be accurately measured and controlled in real time
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the interface between the communication quality control DP and other system components to ensure modularity and ease of integration.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses network gateway and communication management functionalities, including monitoring and control of communication quality.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive network performance data and send status updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the underlying network for monitoring packet loss and adapting control mechanisms.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as communication quality monitoring and adaptive control logically belong to network management within NGHE. Low diffusion since most related data and control are internal or via network interfaces.
Vehicle module is broader and less focused on communication internals, leading to moderate containment but higher diffusion as it would need to interact with network components externally.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control tasks; embedding communication quality control here would cause significant diffusion and cross-cutting concerns.
Safety Services is focused on safety alerts and monitoring, not communication quality control, resulting in poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing communication quality monitoring and adaptive control, as it directly manages network communication and can encapsulate the DP with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if communication quality impacts teleoperation performance, requiring clear interface definitions.
Dependency on accurate telemetry data from external sources may cause coupling outside the module.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a communication quality monitoring and adaptive control mechanism to maintain packet loss within customer-defined limits, directly supporting teleoperation requirements.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module if communication quality impacts teleoperation performance, requiring clear interface definitions.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating good containment of the DP within this IM boundary. The module encapsulates network gateway and communication management functionalities, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry and Network Link interfaces, ensuring packet loss remains within the defined threshold.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces supporting data exchange and control relevant to communication quality.

recommended_revisions: Define clear interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to manage dependencies and minimize diffusion.
Establish protocols to ensure accurate and timely telemetry data from external sources to reduce coupling risks.
Document acceptance criteria explicitly within the NGHE Solution module to facilitate verification and traceability.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical support, good containment, and feasible verification. The DP addresses the FR effectively, the IM encapsulates the DP with low diffusion risk, and verification is feasible with clear allocation. Although some interface and protocol clarifications are recommended, these are refinements rather than fundamental issues requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clarity and traceability at this level is appropriate before proceeding further.

revise.why: Define clear interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules, establish telemetry data protocols, and document acceptance criteria explicitly to reduce coupling risks and facilitate verification.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently captures the communication quality monitoring and control requirements.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate architectural boundary for implementing the DP.
open_risks: Potential overlap and interface diffusion with Teleoperation module if communication quality impacts teleoperation performance.
Dependency on accurate and timely telemetry data from external sources.
evidence_needed: Interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
Protocols for telemetry data acquisition and validation.
Acceptance criteria documentation within NGHE Solution module.
